rationale.R1.7: The delegation 'Check the citations' is exclusively citation-scoped: verify that each in-text cite (Gray & Suri 2019, Kocmi et al. 2022, Papineni et al. 2002, Rei et al. 2020, Kowalski et al. 2019, Liu & Tang 2024) is accurate, exists, and is correctly attributed. Notably, the artifact attributes the 'original COMET formulation' to Kowalski et al. (2019) while separately citing Rei et al. (2020) for COMET — an internal inconsistency that falls squarely within this dimension. This is the load-bearing responsibility; failure to catch attribution errors invalidates the delegation.
rationale.R1.3: Peripherally relevant: verifying whether a cited source actually supports the claim the text makes (e.g., whether Kowalski et al. 2019 truly showed neural metrics are sensitive to reference quality) edges into claim-source alignment, but the delegation does not explicitly request broad evidence auditing — scope is citation fidelity, not internal experimental support.
